intro.title: REACTIVITY BELONGS TO SCOPE.
intro.description: Zenith keeps reactivity attached to the file and markup that own it. Use state for DOM-facing updates, keep slot expressions resolved against parent scope, and avoid hidden component boundaries deciding what is reactive.

demo.title: One example, two surfaces.
demo.description: Flip between the authored Zenith source and the same mounted live counter. The outer shell never remounts; only the inner surface changes.
demo.liveDescription: This button is owned by this file scope. Clicking it updates the bound text in place without creating a separate component-owned reactive scope.
demo.interactiveDescription: The same authored pattern from the code view, presented as a tactile control.
demo.ownershipDescription: This count belongs to the current file scope. If this markup were passed through a slot, parent expressions would still resolve against the parent binding.

principles.label: State Owns Local DOM
principles.title: Use state when the value directly drives markup you own.
principles.description: The binding stays in the current file scope and updates the exact DOM surface it controls.

principles.label: Slots Preserve Parent Scope
principles.title: Slot expressions keep the parent binding they came from.
principles.description: Component-local state does not silently rebind slot content to a new internal ownership layer.

principles.label: Components Stay Structural
principles.title: Components shape markup, not hidden reactive zones.
principles.description: Local state or signal affects component-owned markup only unless values are explicitly passed outward.
